articles.title: REACH MILLIONS OF TECH PROFESSIONALS AT SCALE
articles.summary: Over 6 million tech professionals read TLDR including developers, product managers, marketers, designers and executives. Get in front of your target audience with an ad placement just like this one! Learn more [4] about running a test campaign.

articles.title: WHY BUYING HAS GOTTEN EASIER AND SELLING HAS GOTTEN HARDER
articles.summary: 83% of buyers say purchasing software has gotten easier with bigger budgets and market consolidation, but closing deals is still difficult due to eroding trust. 58% of buyers now start research with AI tools, and most evaluate four or more vendors. The biggest gaps vendors face are delivering credible, relevant proof. 67% of buyers have ruled out a vendor because evidence felt untrustworthy, and younger buyers prefer live references and authentic stories over case studies.

articles.title: AMERICANS HAVE MIXED FEELINGS ABOUT AI SUMMARIES IN SEARCH RESULTS
articles.summary: Pew Research found that a majority of US adults (65%) report coming across AI summaries in search results at least sometimes, and 45% say they see them extremely often or often. Americans are divided on their usefulness, as 20% say they are extremely or very useful, 52% say somewhat useful, and 28% say not useful. Only 6% say they trust the information a lot, while 53% have at least some trust in it.

articles.title: WHATSAPP'S SIGNUP STRATEGY
articles.summary: WhatsApp achieved rapid growth by eliminating friction in signup and making the app feel like an upgrade to texting rather than a new service. Instead of requiring usernames and passwords, it used phone numbers as identification, which allowed seamless onboarding and instant access to contacts. This approach solved the cold start problem by leveraging users' existing social graphs, producing 96% message open rates compared to 20-21% for email. Combined with viral loops built into contact integration, WhatsApp grew to 250,000 users within months, handled 1 billion daily messages by 2011, and reached 200 million active users by early 2013.

articles.title: HOW DATABRICKS TRANSFORMED A PRIVATE SUBREDDIT INTO A POWERFUL COMMUNITY ENGINE
articles.summary: Databricks turned its abandoned subreddit into a multidepartment growth engine by reclaiming ownership, opening it to the public, and creating guardrails for healthy discussions. The community now has 20,000 members, 450 weekly contributions, and drives 10,000+ organic visits per month while ranking for over 300 high-intent keywords. By combining experienced moderators, employee participation, and external advocates, it created a credible and authentic space where users troubleshoot, share objections, and provide real-time feedback. This approach shows how a branded subreddit can function as a strategic asset that strengthens reputation, drives insights, and supports growth across an organization.

articles.title: DRIVING QUALIFIED LEADS WITH LINKEDIN CONTENT: A PROVEN FORMULA
articles.summary: A LinkedIn content formula using niche problem posts generated 10K dollars in pipeline in just 8 days. These 300-word text-only posts follow a simple structure with a hook, symptoms, named problem, solution, proof, and call to action. The strategy relies on real customer language extracted from sales transcripts using AI tools. Posts are designed for mobile with short paragraphs and bolded keywords to improve readability. Each post focuses on one pain point, such as spray and pray outreach, and includes three to five symptoms followed by a named issue and outcomes. Effective calls to action include DM offers or direct links, and the top-performing posts are promoted as paid ads.

articles.title: AI SEARCH OPTIMIZATION MASTERCLASS ON SURFER
articles.summary: This free course includes 9 modules and can be completed in under 2 hours. Lessons cover making a brand recognizable to AI, using schema and entity signals to improve AI understanding, measuring visibility in AI search, and more. A Surfer account is required to enroll, which is free to create.

articles.title: META: INTRODUCING BUSINESS AI: EMPOWERING EVERY BUSINESS TO GROW IN THE AI ERA
articles.summary: Meta launched Business AI, a sales-focused AI agent designed to boost conversions, customer engagement, and long-term relationships across websites, ads, and messaging apps. Early adopters report strong results, including Julep seeing a 13% increase in return on ad spend and a 10% lift in conversions, while Solgaard achieved 6x higher sales conversion rates. Business AI can be set up quickly using existing posts and campaigns, operates 24/7, and is free on Meta Ads.

articles.title: A24 MARKETING STRATEGY: FROM INDIE DARLING TO CULTURAL ICON
articles.summary: A24 built a cultural brand by rejecting traditional Hollywood marketing in favor of low-cost tactics tailored to digitally native cinephiles. Instead of mass reach, it prioritizes high-impact community-building through curated film selection and immersive campaigns. Examples include a Tinder bot for Ex Machina, a pop-up shop for Uncut Gems, and a NYSE matchmaking activation for Materialists. A24 also monetizes directly with the AAA24 membership and branded retail.

articles.title: EVERYBODY'S IN THE PROTEIN GAME
articles.summary: Protein has moved from a fitness niche to a mainstream focus. It's driven largely by diet-conscious Gen Z and millennials. Many are actively trying to increase intake, starting trends like a cottage cheese comeback. Brands are responding with a wide range of products, from frozen cod to GLP-1–friendly options, while introducing new flavors, partnerships, and campaigns that make protein both healthy and appealing.

articles.title: AI IS THE NEW SOCIAL MEDIA
articles.summary: Sora 2 reflects a broader shift to AI-generated social feeds, as 71% of social media images are already AI-generated without users noticing.

articles.title: HOW TO BUILD YOUR OWN GHOSTWRITER WITH CUSTOM GPT
articles.summary: How to build a GPT with step-by-step instructions.

articles.title: SPOTIFY FOR CREATORS: THE FUTURE IS VIDEO
articles.summary: 72% of podcast listeners prefer shows with videos.
